keyRules: Maximum flight altitude: 120 meters (400 ft) above ground level
Your UAV must always remain within your line of sight (VLOS)
Daytime flight only — a separate permit is required for night flights
At least 9 km from all airports (including Istanbul IST and Sabiha Gökçen SAW)
A horizontal distance of at least 50 meters must be maintained from other people
All UAVs weighing more than 500 g must be registered at iha.shgm.gov.tr
Flying is prohibited in historic areas (Hagia Sophia, Topkapı, Sultanahmet) and along the Bosphorus corridor
Flying over military zones, government buildings, and crowded events is strictly prohibited
The use of UAVs weighing more than 500 g by foreign pilots requires an application through diplomatic channels
FPV flight is prohibited without a separate authorization
importantNotes: ⚠️ FOREIGN PILOTS: To operate a drone weighing more than 500 grams in Turkey, you must apply to the General Directorate of Civil Aviation (SHGM) through diplomatic channels at least 20 business days before the flight, obtain insurance, and fly under the supervision of a Turkish citizen holding an İHA-2 license. Drones weighing less than 500 g (such as the DJI Mini, Flip, etc.) are exempt from these requirements.
💰 PENALTY WARNING: Flying without a registration will result in a fine of approximately 78,701 TL. Fines increase annually in line with the revaluation rate; equipment may be confiscated.
🗺️ ZONE SYSTEM: Turkey is divided into green (open), yellow (permit required), and red (prohibited) zones. Be sure to check the zone status at iha.shgm.gov.tr before your flight.

🏛️ HISTORIC AND TOURISTIC SITES: Flying is prohibited over the Historic Peninsula of Istanbul (Sultanahmet, Hagia Sophia, Topkapı, Grand Bazaar), certain valleys in Cappadocia, Pamukkale, Ephesus, and other archaeological sites without special permission from the Ministry of Culture and Tourism.

🛂 IMPORTS AND CUSTOMS: If you are entering Turkey with drones weighing more than 500 g, you may be required to declare them at customs. Imports for commercial purposes require a separate process; the Bosphorus, military ports, and border regions are subject to additional restrictions.

primaryLinks.title: SHGM — Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (official website)
primaryLinks.description: The General Directorate of Civil Aviation's official UAV page: SHT-UAV Directive, weight classes (UAV-0/1/2/3), and operational rules

primaryLinks.title: UAV Registration System (Required for drones weighing 500 g or more)
primaryLinks.description: Mandatory registration portal for all drones weighing more than 500 grams. Requires a Turkish ID number; Form FR-03 applies to foreign nationals

primaryLinks.title: UAV Pilot Registration Application (e-Government)
primaryLinks.description: Official service via e-Government for pilot registration applications for UAV-1 and higher. A pilot license is required for commercial operations.

primaryLinks.title: Flight Authorization Request (yellow/red zones)
primaryLinks.description: In the yellow and red zones, prior authorization from the SHGM is required before flying. In the green zones, compliance with the basic rules is sufficient.

secondaryLinks.title: UAV Tracking — Official Mobile App (Android)
secondaryLinks.description: SHGM's official UAV Tracking app: airspace zones, flight permits, and pilot license management

secondaryLinks.title: SHGM — Corporate Homepage
secondaryLinks.description: General Directorate of Civil Aviation homepage: all aviation regulations, announcements, and contact information

secondaryLinks.title: SHT-UAV Directive (text of the regulation)
secondaryLinks.description: Civil Aviation Directive — Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (SHT-UAV): the official directive that serves as the legal basis for all operations

fields.disclaimerText: Be sure to check the SHGM’s official sources before flying. This app is a tool, not a legal authority. UAV operations in Turkey are regulated by the SHT-UAV Directive; all devices weighing 500 g or more must be registered in the SHGM registration system (iha.shgm.gov.tr).
